calculated_metrics.confidence_note: This unit cost is estimated by PRISM by dividing total programme expenditure by the total quantified animal beneficiaries.

details.calculation: ($1,731,917 USD / 267,100 total beneficiaries) = $6.48 USD per outcome. | A $1,000 USD donation achieves ≈ 154 outcomes.

calculated_metrics.name: Cost Per Outcome (USD)

details.elaboration: Quote: 'In 2024, our world-leading programme reached a new record—267,100 street dogs and cats received life-saving vaccinations and spay/neuter surgeries.'
details.formula: Highest level of self-reported evidence cited in impact metrics

details.calculation: No quantified counterfactual baseline was provided.
details.formula: Presence of a quantified counterfactual baseline

details.calculation: Financials with income breakdown are missing.
Operates in a low-neglectedness / saturated area (Companion Animals: 100%).
details.formula: Proportional analysis of beneficiary populations (farmed/wild vs. companion)

details.calculation: No disclosure of unintended negative impacts found. This is the industry norm.
details.elaboration: This check rewards epistemic humility. Non-disclosure is standard and not a failure.
details.formula: Check for self-reported unintended consequences or failures.

details.calculation: No disclosure of euthanasia or live-release rates found.
details.elaboration: This check rewards transparency for a sensitive operational metric.
details.formula: For direct rescue orgs, check for euthanasia/live-release rate disclosure.

source.quote: ADMINISTRATION $943,856
FUNDRAISING $5,549,574
CAPITAL EXPENDITURE & LAND PURCHASE $922,583
ADVOCACY & MARKETING $303,160
ANIMAL WELFARE PROGRAMMES $13,450,737
TOTAL EXPENDITURE $21,169,910
GLOBAL INCOME $22,409,766
TOTAL EXPENDITURE $21,169,910
In 2024, our world-leading programme reached a new record—267,100 street dogs and cats received life-saving vaccinations and spay/neuter surgeries.

TO IMPROVE THE WELFARE OF DOGS AND CATS IN ASIA, RESULTING IN BETTER LIVES FOR BOTH THE ANIMAL AND HUMAN COMMUNITIES, TO CREATE A SOCIETY WITHOUT HOMELESS ANIMALS, AND TO ULTIMATELY END ANIMAL CRUELTY.

metrics.context_qualifier: Street dogs and cats received life-saving vaccinations and spay/neuter surgeries through the CNVR programme.

source.quote: In 2024, our world-leading programme reached a new record—267,100 street dogs and cats received life-saving vaccinations and spay/neuter surgeries.

metrics.context_qualifier: Dogs and cats received critical medical care, rehabilitation, and comfort, representing a 6% increase from the previous year.

source.quote: This year, we treated 22,736 dogs and cats—an increase of 6%, delivering critical medical care, rehabilitation, and comfort to animals in need.

metrics.context_qualifier: Total number of animals vaccinated and spayed/neutered since the programme's inception in 2003.

source.quote: 1,355,495 animals vaccinated, spayed/neutered since 2003.

metrics.context_qualifier: Children engaged through educational sessions and events, inspiring them to become compassionate advocates for animals.

source.quote: The team also engaged 1,885 children through educational sessions and events, inspiring them to become compassionate advocates for animals.

metrics.context_qualifier: Total animals under the care of Soi Dog, including residents, patients, and those in temporary foster care, by year-end.

source.quote: By the close of the year, 1,877 animals were under the care of Soi Dog, which includes residents, patients and those in temporary foster care.

metrics.context_qualifier: Front office staff managed calls for help, representing a 4% rise over the previous year.

source.quote: our front office staff managed 17,714 calls for help—a 4% rise over the previous year.

metrics.context_qualifier: Tonnes of food distributed to a network of volunteer feeders across the island to support stray animals.

source.quote: 135.86 tonnes of food distributed to feeders across the island

metrics.context_qualifier: Total food provided for animals affected by severe flooding in northern and southern Thailand.
metrics.metric_name: Total Food Provided for Animals Affected by Floods

source.quote: 43.599 tonnes of food provided for animals affected by the floods

metrics.context_qualifier: Dog and cat food delivered through fundraising efforts during disaster response.

source.quote: 36.097 tonnes of dog and cat food delivered through fundraising

metrics.context_qualifier: Dog and cat food received as in-kind donations during disaster response.

source.quote: 7.502 tonnes of dog and cat food received as in-kind donations

metrics.context_qualifier: Veterinary surgeons performed life-saving operations for dogs and cats in need.

source.quote: Behind the scenes, our veterinary surgeons performed 1,038 life-saving operations

metrics.context_qualifier: Rescued dogs and cats left the Soi Dog shelter for loving homes, both within Thailand and abroad.

source.quote: A total of 461 rescued animals - 308 dogs and 153 cats - left the Soi Dog shelter for loving homes in 2024.

metrics.context_qualifier: Social change team launched investigations into animal cruelty cases.

source.quote: 353 investigations launched

metrics.context_qualifier: Participants trained through 7 feeder workshops to educate on responsible care and first aid.

source.quote: 145 participants trained through 7 feeder workshops

metrics.context_qualifier: Dedicated network of volunteer feeders supported monthly to ensure animals received food and care.

source.quote: 151 feeders supported monthly

metrics.context_qualifier: Dogs successfully rehabilitated to overcome past trauma and prepare for adoption.

source.quote: Thanks to your generous support, they successfully rehabilitated 78 dogs, helping them to overcome past trauma, learn to trust, and develop the confidence needed to thrive in homes.

metrics.context_qualifier: Dogs rescued from the meat trade in the Philippines, in partnership with Animal Kingdom Foundation.

source.quote: Alongside our trusted partners, Animal Kingdom Foundation, we rescued 24 dogs from the meat trade, including Basti (pictured above), who is now thriving in his loving home.

metrics.context_qualifier: Awareness billboards installed across Phuket to raise awareness on animal cruelty.

source.quote: 15 awareness billboards installed in Phuket

metrics.context_qualifier: Arrests of dog meat traders secured in the Philippines, sending a strong message against animal cruelty.

source.quote: We also helped secure 10 arrests and 2 convictions of dog meat traders, sending a strong message: animal cruelty will not go unpunished.

metrics.context_qualifier: Prosecutions initiated under the Animal Welfare Act as part of efforts to tackle animal cruelty.

source.quote: 4 prosecutions under the Animal Welfare Act

metrics.context_qualifier: Convictions of dog meat traders secured in the Philippines, reinforcing legal action against animal cruelty.

source.quote: We also helped secure 10 arrests and 2 convictions of dog meat traders, sending a strong message: animal cruelty will not go unpunished.

significant_events.event_name: Dog and Cat Meat Trade Systemic Change

source.quote: 2024 marked a landmark year in our fight to end the dog and cat meat trade. Together, we achieved systemic change in Vietnam and the Philippines—securing legislative amendments, conducting slaughterhouse raids, and saving lives.

significant_events.summary: Achieved systemic change in Vietnam and the Philippines, securing legislative amendments, conducting slaughterhouse raids, and saving lives.

significant_events.event_name: CNVR (Catch, Neuter, Vaccinate and Return) Programme

source.quote: In 2024, our world-leading programme reached a new record—267,100 street dogs and cats received life-saving vaccinations and spay/neuter surgeries.

significant_events.summary: World-leading programme reached a new record—267,100 street dogs and cats received life-saving vaccinations and spay/neuter surgeries.

source.quote: When unprecedented flooding struck, we acted without hesitation, delivering over 43 tonnes of food to animals in crisis—all thanks to your unwavering support.

significant_events.summary: Acted swiftly when disaster struck due to torrential rains, delivering over 43 tonnes of food to animals in crisis in northern and southern Thailand.

source.quote: In 2024, our Humane Education programme continued to engage young minds, fostering a deep understanding of animal welfare and responsible pet ownership.

significant_events.summary: Continued to engage young minds, fostering understanding of animal welfare and responsible pet ownership, formalizing partnership with Phuket Primary Education Office.

source.quote: In September, we proudly launched our 19th mobile CNVR clinic, joining two others already stationed in Surat Thani, Thailand's sixth-largest province.

significant_events.summary: Launched the 19th mobile CNVR clinic in September, expanding capacity to improve lives of street animals across Thailand.

source.quote: Guided by our expert teams, 62 veterinary students gained hands-on CNVR experience, while another 96 embraced the opportunity to delve into shelter medicine through our exclusive course in partnership with the Faculty of Veterinary Medicine at Chulalongkorn University.

significant_events.summary: 62 veterinary students gained hands-on CNVR experience, and 96 delved into shelter medicine through a course with Chulalongkorn University.

source.quote: This momentum carried through to November, when Bangkok became one of the first cities to join the Positive Cities Pledge—a global commitment to humane and sustainable stray dog population management.

significant_events.summary: Bangkok became one of the first cities to join the Positive Cities Pledge, committing to humane and sustainable stray dog population management.

significant_events.event_name: Soi Dog Becomes Legal Entity in Vietnam

source.quote: Our biggest milestone came in November, when Soi Dog Foundation officially became a legal entity in Vietnam after receiving an operating registration certificate, achieved in a record-breaking time frame of just seven months.

significant_events.summary: Soi Dog Foundation officially became a legal entity in Vietnam after receiving an operating registration certificate in a record-breaking seven months.

significant_events.event_name: Calzone Adopted by Thai Royal Family

source.quote: August brought one of the year's most memorable moments: the adoption of a Soi Dog into none other than the Thai royal family.

significant_events.summary: A rescued Soi Dog, Calzone, was adopted into the Thai royal family, marking a memorable moment for the foundation.

source.quote: By the close of the year, 1,877 animals were under the care of Soi Dog, which includes residents, patients and those in temporary foster care.

Of course, 2024 was not without its challenges. The ongoing ban on dog imports into Canada, combined with the global cost of living crisis, continued to impact adoption numbers, leading to an 11% decrease compared to the previous year.

data.original_text: Soi Dog's CNVR programme – the largest of its kind in the world today - is targeted at reducing the number of animals born into a life of suffering and abuse or neglect, eliminating rabies and stopping the spread of other infectious and deadly diseases among stray dogs and cats.

As part of a wider community engagement initiative, Soi Dog runs humane education, community liaison and outreach programmes targeted to help improve the welfare of both stray and domestic animals, educating local schoolchildren and the wider public on animal welfare whilst providing support to network of volunteer 'feeders', caring members of the local community who act as guardians for stray animals.

Soi Dog campaigns for an improvement in animal welfare rights across Asia and was the driving force behind the promulgation of Thailand's first-ever animal welfare act introduced in 2014, bringing in new laws specifically covering cruelty to animals.
The Soi Dog Foundation Gill Dalley Sanctuary in Phuket welcomes visitors and volunteers from all over the world, year-round.
For animals unable to be returned safely to the streets, Soi Dog provides sanctuary - a haven where sick, injured and vulnerable dogs and cats receive world-class medical care, and love.

Soi Dog oversees local, national and international adoptions, directly rehoming thousands of dogs and cats rescued from dire situations across the region, from government shelters, and from the horrendous dog and cat meat trade.
Soi Dog Foundation USA supports and promotes the animal welfare efforts of Soi Dog Foundation, a legally registered charitable organization based in Phuket, Thailand.

Soi Dog Foundation mission statement: Improve the welfare of dogs and cats in Asia, resulting in better lives for both the animal and human communities, to create a society without homeless animals and to ultimately end animal cruelty.
Target demographics: Support and promote the efforts of Soi Dog Foundation in Southeast Asia.

Direct beneficiaries per year: Neuter and vaccinate almost 200,000 dogs and cats, provide veterinary treatment to over 17,000 animals, deliver hundreds of tons of food and urgent supplies to tens of thousands of stricken animals affected by natural and man-made disasters, save dogs and cats from the meat trade in the Philippines.
The majority of Soi Dog's work is focused on an holistic approach including Rescue, Sterilization, Vaccination, Medical Treatment, Shelter and Adoption.
Community Outreach and Education Outreach are also integral parts of their programs.

Soi Dog's teams of animal rescue officers, coordinated by a hospital office team, travel the island of Phuket and nearby province of Phang Nga to respond to animal emergencies and reports of stray dogs or cats requiring hospital treatment.
In 2024, our world-leading programme reached a new record—267,100 street dogs and cats received life-saving vaccinations and spay/neuter surgeries.
A total of 461 rescued animals – 308 dogs and 153 cats – left the Soi Dog shelter for loving homes in 2024.
Soi Dog Foundation has almost eradicated rabies from Thailand.
Phuket in particular has been declared rabies free – such is the power of vaccination.
By the close of the year [2024], 1,877 animals were under the care of Soi Dog, which includes residents, patients and those in temporary foster care.
